en: Directly after this séance S. W. became acquainted with the book of Justinus Kerner, "Die Seherin von Prevorst." She began thereupon to magnetise herself towards the end of the attack, partly by means of regular passes, partly by curious circles and figures of eight, which she described symmetrically with both arms. She did this, she said, to disperse the severe headaches which occurred after the attacks. In the August séances, not detailed here, there were in addition to the grandfather numerous spirits of other relatives who did not produce anything very remarkable. Each time when a new one came on the scene the movement of the glass was changed in a striking way; it generally ran along the rows of letters, touching one or other of them, but no sense could be made of it. The orthography was very uncertain and arbitrary, and the first sentences were frequently incomprehensible or broken up into a meaningless medley of letters. Generally automatic writing suddenly began at this point. Sometimes automatic writing was attempted during complete darkness. The movements began with violent backward jerks of the whole arm, so that the paper was pierced by the pencil. The first attempt at writing consisted of numerous strokes and zigzag lines about 8 cm. high. In later attempts there came first unreadable words, in large handwriting, which gradually became smaller and clearer. It was not essentially different from the medium's own. The grandfather was again the controlling spirit.
zh: 緊接在這場降靈會之後，S. W. 認識了尤斯提努斯·克爾納的著作《普雷沃斯特的女先知》。她於是開始在發作接近尾聲時對自己進行磁化，一部分是透過規律的劃手動作，一部分是透過奇特的圓圈和八字形，她用雙臂對稱地描繪這些圖形。她這麼做，是為了緩解發作後出現的劇烈頭痛。在八月的降靈會中（此處未詳述），除了祖父之外，還有許多其他親屬的靈體出現，但並未產生任何非常顯著的事蹟。每當有新靈體登場時，玻璃杯的移動方式就會以驚人的方式改變；它通常沿著字母行移動，觸碰其中一個或另一個字母，但無法從中解讀出任何意義。拼字非常不確定且任意，最初的句子常常難以理解，或碎裂成毫無意義的字母組合。通常，自動書寫會在此時突然開始。有時，自動書寫的嘗試是在完全黑暗的環境中進行的。動作始於整隻手臂猛烈的向後抽搐，以致鉛筆刺穿了紙張。最初的書寫嘗試由大量約 8 公分高的筆畫和鋸齒線條組成。在後續的嘗試中，首先出現的是難以辨認的文字，字跡很大，然後逐漸變小、變清晰。它與靈媒自己的字跡並無本質上的差異。祖父再次成為主導的靈體。
